BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResource object { memory\_store\_id, type, access, 4 more } Óāö

A memory store attached to an agent session.

memory\_store\_id: stringÓāö

The memory store ID (memstore\_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

type: "memory\_store"Óāö

ÓÄ™

access: optional "read\_write" or "read\_only"Óāö

Access mode for an attached memory store.

One of the following:

"read\_write"Óāö

"read\_only"Óāö

description: optional stringÓāö

Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.

instructions: optional stringÓāö

Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.

mount\_path: optional stringÓāö

Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.

name: optional stringÓāö

Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.

#### BetaSessionsThreads

##### [List Session Threads](api/beta/sessions/threads/list.md)

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads

##### [Get Session Thread](api/beta/sessions/threads/retrieve.md)

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}

##### [Archive Session Thread](api/beta/sessions/threads/archive.md)

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}/archive

BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEvent object { id, result, tool\_use\_id, 4 more } Óāö

A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.

result: "allow" or "deny"Óāö

UserToolConfirmationResult enum

One of the following:

"allow"Óāö

"deny"Óāö

tool\_use\_id: stringÓāö

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

type: "user.tool\_confirmation"Óāö

deny\_message: optional stringÓāö

Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.

processed\_at: optional stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringÓāö

When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.

Routes this result to a subagent thread. Copy from the `agent.custom_tool_use` event's `session_thread_id`.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsAgentCustomToolUseEvent object { id, input, name, 3 more } Óāö

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

input: map[unknown]Óāö

Input parameters for the tool call.

name: stringÓāö

Name of the custom tool being called.

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "agent.custom\_tool\_use"Óāö

session\_thread\_id: optional stringÓāö

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.

ÓÄ™

Array of text blocks comprising the agent response.

The text content.

type: "text"Óāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "agent.message"Óāö

BetaManagedAgentsSessionErrorEvent object { id, error, processed\_at, type } Óāö

An error event indicating a problem occurred during session execution.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

ÓÄ™

error: [BetaManagedAgentsUnknownError](api/beta.md) { message, retry\_status, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsModelOverloadedError](api/beta.md) { message, retry\_status, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsModelRateLimitedError](api/beta.md) { message, retry\_status, type }  or 5 moreÓāö

An unknown or unexpected error occurred during session execution. A fallback variant; clients that don't recognize a new error code can match on `retry_status` and `message` alone.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUnknownError object { message, retry\_status, type } Óāö

An unknown or unexpected error occurred during session execution. A fallback variant; clients that don't recognize a new error code can match on `retry_status` and `message` alone.

message: stringÓāö

Human-readable error description.

ÓÄ™

retry\_status: [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminal](api/beta.md) { type } Óāö

What the client should do next in response to this error.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying object { type } Óāö

BetaManagedAgentsSessionEndTurn object { type } Óāö

The agent completed its turn naturally and is ready for the next user message.

BetaManagedAgentsSessionRequiresAction object { event\_ids, type } Óāö

The agent is idle waiting on one or more blocking user-input events (tool confirmation, custom tool result, etc.). Resolving all of them transitions the session back to running.

event\_ids: array of stringÓāö

The ids of events the agent is blocked on. Resolving fewer than all re-emits `session.status_idle` with the remainder.

type: "requires\_action"Óāö

ÓÄ™

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "session.status\_terminated"Óāö

BetaManagedAgentsSessionThreadCreatedEvent object { id, agent\_name, processed\_at, 2 more } Óāö

Emitted when a subagent is spawned as a new thread. Written to the parent thread's output stream so clients observing the session see child creation.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

agent\_name: stringÓāö

Name of the callable agent the thread runs.

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: stringÓāö

Public `sthr_` ID of the newly created thread.

type: "session.thread\_created"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSpanOutcomeEvaluationStartEvent object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } Óāö

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle begins.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberÓāö

0-indexed revision cycle. 0 is the first evaluation; 1 is the re-evaluation after the first revision; etc.

outcome\_id: stringÓāö

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSpanOutcomeEvaluationEndEvent object { id, explanation, iteration, 6 more } Óāö

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle completes. Carries the verdict and aggregate token usage. A verdict of `needs_revision` means another evaluation cycle follows; `satisfied`, `max_iterations_reached`, `failed`, or `interrupted` are terminal ‚ÄĒ no further evaluation cycles follow.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

explanation: stringÓāö

Human-readable explanation of the verdict. For `needs_revision`, describes which criteria failed and why.

iteration: numberÓāö

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_evaluation\_start\_id: stringÓāö

The id of the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` event.

outcome\_id: stringÓāö

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

result: stringÓāö

Evaluation verdict. 'satisfied': criteria met, session goes idle. 'needs\_revision': criteria not met, another revision cycle follows. 'max\_iterations\_reached': evaluation budget exhausted with criteria still unmet ‚ÄĒ one final acknowledgment turn follows before the session goes idle, but no further evaluation runs. 'failed': grader determined the rubric does not apply to the deliverables. 'interrupted': user sent an interrupt while evaluation was in progress.

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"Óāö

ÓÄ™

usage: [BetaManagedAgentsSpanModelUsage](api/beta.md) { cache\_creation\_input\_tokens, cache\_read\_input\_tokens, input\_tokens, 2 more } Óāö

Token usage for a single model request.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: numberÓāö

Tokens used to create prompt cache in this request.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: numberÓāö

Tokens read from prompt cache in this request.

input\_tokens: numberÓāö

Input tokens consumed by this request.

output\_tokens: numberÓāö

Output tokens generated by this request.

ÓÄ™

speed: optional "standard" or "fast"Óāö

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

One of the following:

"standard"Óāö

"fast"Óāö

ÓÄ™

Emitted when a model request is initiated by the agent.

Unique identifier for this event.

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "span.model\_request\_start"Óāö

ÓÄ™

Emitted when a model request completes.

Unique identifier for this event.

is\_error: booleanÓāö

Whether the model request resulted in an error.

model\_request\_start\_id: stringÓāö

The id of the corresponding `span.model_request_start` event.

ÓÄ™

model\_usage: [BetaManagedAgentsSpanModelUsage](api/beta.md) { cache\_creation\_input\_tokens, cache\_read\_input\_tokens, input\_tokens, 2 more } Óāö

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: numberÓāö

Tokens used to create prompt cache in this request.

Input tokens consumed by this request.

output\_tokens: numberÓāö

Output tokens generated by this request.

ÓÄ™

speed: optional "standard" or "fast"Óāö

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

One of the following:

"standard"Óāö

"fast"Óāö

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "span.model\_request\_end"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSpanOutcomeEvaluationOngoingEvent object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } Óāö

Periodic heartbeat emitted while an outcome evaluation cycle is in progress. Distinguishes 'evaluation is actively running' from 'evaluation is stuck' between the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` and `span.outcome_evaluation_end` events.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberÓāö

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_id: stringÓāö

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUserDefineOutcomeEvent object { id, description, max\_iterations, 4 more } Óāö

Echo of a `user.define_outcome` input event. Carries the server-generated `outcome_id` that subsequent `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events reference.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this event.

description: stringÓāö

What the agent should produce. Copied from the input event.

max\_iterations: numberÓāö

Evaluate-then-revise cycles before giving up. Default 3, max 20.

outcome\_id: stringÓāö

Server-generated `outc_` ID for this outcome. Referenced by `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events and the session's `outcome_evaluations` list.

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

ÓÄ™

rubric: [BetaManagedAgentsFileRubric](api/beta.md) { file\_id, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsTextRubric](api/beta.md) { content, type } Óāö

file\_id: stringÓāö

type: "file"Óāö

ÓÄ™

Rubric content provided inline as text.

Rubric content. Plain text or markdown ‚ÄĒ the grader treats it as freeform text.

type: "text"Óāö

processed\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

#### BetaSessionsThreadsEvents

##### [List Session Thread Events](api/beta/sessions/threads/events/list.md)

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}/events

##### [Stream Session Thread Events](api/beta/sessions/threads/events/stream.md)

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}/stream

#### BetaDeployments

##### [Create Deployment](api/beta/deployments/create.md)

POST/v1/deployments

##### [List Deployments](api/beta/deployments/list.md)

GET/v1/deployments

##### [Get Deployment](api/beta/deployments/retrieve.md)

GET/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}

##### [Update Deployment](api/beta/deployments/update.md)

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}

##### [Archive Deployment](api/beta/deployments/archive.md)

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/archive

##### [Run Deployment Now](api/beta/deployments/run.md)

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/run

##### [Pause Deployment](api/beta/deployments/pause.md)

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/pause

##### [Unpause Deployment](api/beta/deployments/unpause.md)

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/unpause

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsAgentArchivedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

The deployment's agent was archived.

type: "agent\_archived\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsCronSchedule object { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } Óāö

5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.

expression: stringÓāö

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringÓāö

IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC").

type: "cron"Óāö

last\_run\_at: optional stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

upcoming\_runs\_at: optional array of stringÓāö

Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsCronScheduleParams object { expression, timezone, type } Óāö

5-field POSIX cron schedule. Literal wall-clock matching in the configured timezone.

expression: stringÓāö

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringÓāö

Required. IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC"). Validated against the IANA timezone database.

type: "cron"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsDeployment object { id, agent, archived\_at, 13 more } Óāö

A deployment is a configured instance of an agent ‚ÄĒ it binds the agent to everything needed to run it autonomously: an environment, credentials, initial events, and an optional schedule.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this deployment.

ÓÄ™

version: numberÓāö

archived\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

created\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

description: stringÓāö

Description of what the deployment does.

environment\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the `environment` where sessions run.

BetaManagedAgentsDeploymentSystemMessageEvent object { content, type } Óāö

Privileged context for the accompanying turn and all subsequent turns, appended to the session's system context as a `role: "system"` turn rather than replacing the top-level system prompt.

type: "system.message"Óāö

metadata: map[string]Óāö

Arbitrary key-value metadata. Maximum 16 pairs.

name: stringÓāö

Human-readable name.

ÓÄ™

paused\_reason: [BetaManagedAgentsDeploymentPausedReason](api/beta.md)Óāö

Why a deployment is paused. Non-null exactly when `status` is `paused`.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsManualDeploymentPausedReason object { type } Óāö

The caller invoked the pause endpoint on the deployment.

type: "manual"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsErrorDeploymentPausedReason object { error, type } Óāö

A scheduled fire recorded a failed run whose error auto-pauses the deployment.

ÓÄ™

error: [BetaManagedAgentsDeploymentPausedReasonError](api/beta.md)Óāö

The error that triggered an auto-pause. Matches the failed run's `error.type`.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsEnvironmentArchivedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

The deployment's environment was archived.

type: "environment\_archived\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsAgentArchivedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

The deployment's agent was archived.

type: "agent\_archived\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsEnvironmentNotFoundDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

The deployment's environment no longer exists.

type: "environment\_not\_found\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsVaultNotFoundDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

A vault referenced by the deployment no longer exists.

type: "vault\_not\_found\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsFileNotFoundDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

A file resource referenced by the deployment no longer exists.

type: "file\_not\_found\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSessionResourceNotFoundDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

A referenced resource no longer exists and its kind was not reported.

type: "session\_resource\_not\_found\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsWorkspaceArchivedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

The deployment's workspace was archived.

type: "workspace\_archived\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsOrganizationDisabledDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

The deployment's organization is disabled.

type: "organization\_disabled\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreArchivedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

A memory store referenced by the deployment is archived.

type: "memory\_store\_archived\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSkillNotFoundDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

A skill referenced by the deployment's agent no longer exists.

type: "skill\_not\_found\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsVaultArchivedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

A vault referenced by the deployment is archived.

type: "vault\_archived\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUnknownDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

An unrecognized error auto-paused the deployment. A fallback variant; matches a run whose `error.type` is `unknown_error`.

type: "unknown\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSelfHostedResourcesUnsupportedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

The deployment configures resources, but its environment is self-hosted and cannot mount them.

type: "self\_hosted\_resources\_unsupported\_error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMCPEgressBlockedDeploymentPausedReasonError object { type } Óāö

An MCP server host used by the deployment's agent is blocked by the environment's network policy.

type: "mcp\_egress\_blocked\_error"Óāö

type: "error"Óāö

ÓÄ™

resources: array of [BetaManagedAgentsSessionResourceConfig](api/beta.md)Óāö

Resources attached to sessions created from this deployment. Echoes the input minus write-only credentials.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsGitHubRepositoryResourceConfig object { type, url, checkout, mount\_path } Óāö

A GitHub repository mounted into each session's container. The authorization token is write-only and never returned.

type: "github\_repository"Óāö

url: stringÓāö

Github URL of the repository

ÓÄ™

checkout: optional [BetaManagedAgentsBranchCheckout](api/beta.md) { name, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsCommitCheckout](api/beta.md) { sha, type } Óāö

Branch or commit to check out. Defaults to the repository's default branch.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsBranchCheckout object { name, type } Óāö

name: stringÓāö

Branch name to check out.

type: "branch"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsCommitCheckout object { sha, type } Óāö

sha: stringÓāö

Full commit SHA to check out.

type: "commit"Óāö

mount\_path: optional stringÓāö

Mount path in the container. Defaults to `/workspace/<repo-name>`.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsFileResourceConfig object { file\_id, type, mount\_path } Óāö

A file mounted into each session's container.

file\_id: stringÓāö

ID of a previously uploaded file.

type: "file"Óāö

mount\_path: optional stringÓāö

Mount path in the container. Defaults to `/mnt/session/uploads/<file_id>`.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceConfig object { memory\_store\_id, type, access, instructions } Óāö

A memory store attached to each session created from this deployment.

memory\_store\_id: stringÓāö

The memory store ID (memstore\_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

type: "memory\_store"Óāö

ÓÄ™

access: optional "read\_write" or "read\_only"Óāö

Access mode for an attached memory store.

One of the following:

"read\_write"Óāö

"read\_only"Óāö

instructions: optional stringÓāö

Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.

ÓÄ™

schedule: [BetaManagedAgentsSchedule](api/beta.md) { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } Óāö

5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.

expression: stringÓāö

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringÓāö

IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC").

type: "cron"Óāö

last\_run\_at: optional stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

upcoming\_runs\_at: optional array of stringÓāö

Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.

ÓÄ™

status: [BetaManagedAgentsDeploymentStatus](api/beta.md)Óāö

Lifecycle status of a deployment.

One of the following:

"active"Óāö

"paused"Óāö

type: "deployment"Óāö

updated\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

vault\_ids: array of stringÓāö

Vault IDs supplying stored credentials for sessions created from this deployment.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSystemMessageEventParams object { content, type } Óāö

Privileged context for the accompanying turn and all subsequent turns, appended to the session's system context as a `role: "system"` turn rather than replacing the top-level system prompt. At most one per request: it must be the final event and immediately follow the `user.message`, `user.tool_result`, or `user.custom_tool_result` it accompanies. Only supported on models that accept mid-conversation system messages.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSchedule object { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } Óāö

5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.

expression: stringÓāö

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringÓāö

IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC").

type: "cron"Óāö

last\_run\_at: optional stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

upcoming\_runs\_at: optional array of stringÓāö

Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsScheduleParams object { expression, timezone, type } Óāö

5-field POSIX cron schedule. Literal wall-clock matching in the configured timezone.

expression: stringÓāö

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringÓāö

Required. IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC"). Validated against the IANA timezone database.

type: "cron"Óāö

ÓÄ™

#### BetaDeployment Runs

##### [List Deployment Runs](api/beta/deployment_runs/list.md)

GET/v1/deployment\_runs

##### [Get Deployment Run](api/beta/deployment_runs/retrieve.md)

GET/v1/deployment\_runs/{deployment\_run\_id}

#### BetaVaults

GET/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}

##### [Update Vault](api/beta/vaults/update.md)

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}

##### [Delete Vault](api/beta/vaults/delete.md)

DELETE/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}

##### [Archive Vault](api/beta/vaults/archive.md)

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/archive

networking: [BetaManagedAgentsUnrestrictedCredentialNetworkingResponse](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsLimitedCredentialNetworkingResponse](api/beta.md) { allowed\_hosts, type } Óāö

Outbound hosts the secret value is substituted on.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUnrestrictedCredentialNetworkingResponse object { type } Óāö

The secret is substituted on any host the session's Environment network policy permits egress to.

type: "unrestricted"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsLimitedCredentialNetworkingResponse object { allowed\_hosts, type } Óāö

The secret is substituted only on requests to the listed hosts.

allowed\_hosts: array of stringÓāö

Hostnames on which the secret will be substituted. An entry matches the request host exactly; a `*.`-prefixed entry matches any subdomain of the named domain but not the domain itself.

type: "limited"Óāö

secret\_name: stringÓāö

Name of the environment variable.

type: "environment\_variable"Óāö

created\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

metadata: map[string]Óāö

Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the credential.

type: "vault\_credential"Óāö

updated\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

vault\_id: stringÓāö

Identifier of the vault this credential belongs to.

display\_name: optional stringÓāö

Human-readable name for the credential.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsCredentialNetworkingParams = [BetaManagedAgentsUnrestrictedCredentialNetworkingParams](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsLimitedCredentialNetworkingParams](api/beta.md) { allowed\_hosts, type } Óāö

Substitute the secret on any host the session's Environment network policy permits egress to. The Environment's network policy is the only boundary on where the secret can reach.

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUnrestrictedCredentialNetworkingParams object { type } Óāö

Substitute the secret on any host the session's Environment network policy permits egress to. The Environment's network policy is the only boundary on where the secret can reach.

type: "unrestricted"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsLimitedCredentialNetworkingParams object { allowed\_hosts, type } Óāö

Substitute the secret only on requests to the listed hosts.

allowed\_hosts: array of stringÓāö

Hostnames on which the secret will be substituted. Each entry is a bare hostname (`api.example.com`), an IPv4 address (`192.0.2.1`), or a `*.`-prefixed wildcard (`*.example.com`). URLs, ports, paths, and IPv6 addresses are not accepted. At most 16 entries.

type: "limited"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsCredentialValidation object { credential\_id, has\_refresh\_token, mcp\_probe, 5 more } Óāö

Result of live-probing a credential against its configured MCP server.

credential\_id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier of the credential that was validated.

has\_refresh\_token: booleanÓāö

Whether the credential has a refresh token configured.

ÓÄ™

mcp\_probe: [BetaManagedAgentsMCPProbe](api/beta.md) { http\_response, method } Óāö

The failing step of an MCP validation probe.

ÓÄ™

http\_response: [BetaManagedAgentsRefreshHTTPResponse](api/beta.md) { body, body\_truncated, content\_type, status\_code } Óāö

An HTTP response captured during a credential validation probe.

body: stringÓāö

Response body. May be truncated and has sensitive values scrubbed.

body\_truncated: booleanÓāö

Whether `body` was truncated.

content\_type: stringÓāö

Value of the `Content-Type` response header.

status\_code: numberÓāö

HTTP status code.

method: stringÓāö

The MCP method that failed (for example `initialize` or `tools/list`).

ÓÄ™

refresh: [BetaManagedAgentsRefreshObject](api/beta.md) { http\_response, status } Óāö

Outcome of a refresh-token exchange attempted during credential validation.

ÓÄ™

http\_response: [BetaManagedAgentsRefreshHTTPResponse](api/beta.md) { body, body\_truncated, content\_type, status\_code } Óāö

An HTTP response captured during a credential validation probe.

body: stringÓāö

Response body. May be truncated and has sensitive values scrubbed.

body\_truncated: booleanÓāö

Whether `body` was truncated.

content\_type: stringÓāö

status\_code: numberÓāö

HTTP status code.

ÓÄ™

status: "succeeded" or "failed" or "connect\_error" or "no\_refresh\_token"Óāö

Outcome of a refresh-token exchange attempted during credential validation.

"succeeded"Óāö

"failed"Óāö

"connect\_error"Óāö

"no\_refresh\_token"Óāö

ÓÄ™

#### BetaMemory Stores

##### [Create a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/create.md)

POST/v1/memory\_stores

##### [List memory stores](api/beta/memory_stores/list.md)

GET/v1/memory\_stores

##### [Retrieve a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/retrieve.md)

GET/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}

##### [Update a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/update.md)

POST/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}

##### [Delete a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/delete.md)

DELETE/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}

##### [Archive a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/archive.md)

POST/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/archive

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsDeletedMemoryStore object { id, type } Óāö

Confirmation that a `memory_store` was deleted.

id: stringÓāö

ID of the deleted memory store (a `memstore_...` identifier). The store and all its memories and versions are no longer retrievable.

type: "memory\_store\_deleted"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStore object { id, created\_at, name, 5 more } Óāö

A `memory_store`: a named container for agent memories, scoped to a workspace. Attach a store to a session via `resources[]` to mount it as a directory the agent can read and write.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for the memory store (a `memstore_...` tagged ID). Use this when attaching the store to a session, or in the `{memory_store_id}` path parameter of subsequent calls.

created\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

name: stringÓāö

Human-readable name for the store. 1‚Äď255 characters. The store's mount-path slug under `/mnt/memory/` is derived from this name.

type: "memory\_store"Óāö

updated\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

archived\_at: optional stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

description: optional stringÓāö

Free-text description of what the store contains, up to 1024 characters. Included in the agent's system prompt when the store is attached, so word it to be useful to the agent. Empty string when unset.

metadata: optional map[string]Óāö

Arbitrary key-value tags for your own bookkeeping (such as the end user a store belongs to). Up to 16 pairs; keys 1‚Äď64 characters; values up to 512 characters. Returned on retrieve/list but not filterable.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsConflictError object { type, message } Óāö

type: "conflict\_error"Óāö

message: optional stringÓāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsContentSha256Precondition object { type, content\_sha256 } Óāö

Optimistic-concurrency precondition: the update applies only if the memory's stored `content_sha256` equals the supplied value. On mismatch, the request returns `memory_precondition_failed_error` (HTTP 409); re-read the memory and retry against the fresh state. If the precondition fails but the stored state already exactly matches the requested `content` and `path`, the server returns 200 instead of 409.

type: "content\_sha256"Óāö

content\_sha256: optional stringÓāö

Expected `content_sha256` of the stored memory (64 lowercase hexadecimal characters). Typically the `content_sha256` returned by a prior read or list call. Because the server applies no content normalization, clients can also compute this locally as the SHA-256 of the UTF-8 content bytes.

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsDeletedMemory object { id, type } Óāö

Tombstone returned by [Delete a memory](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/delete.md). The memory's version history persists and remains listable via [List memory versions](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list.md) until the store itself is deleted.

id: stringÓāö

ID of the deleted memory (a `mem_...` value).

type: "memory\_deleted"Óāö

message: optional stringÓāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemory object { id, content\_sha256, content\_size\_bytes, 7 more } Óāö

A `memory` object: a single text document at a hierarchical path inside a memory store. The `content` field is populated when `view=full` and `null` when `view=basic`; the `content_size_bytes` and `content_sha256` fields are always populated so sync clients can diff without fetching content. Memories are addressed by their `mem_...` ID; the path is the create key and can be changed via update.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this memory (a `mem_...` value). Stable across renames; use this ID, not the path, to read, update, or delete the memory.

content\_sha256: stringÓāö

Lowercase hex SHA-256 digest of the UTF-8 `content` bytes (64 characters). The server applies no normalization, so clients can compute the same hash locally for staleness checks and as the value for a `content_sha256` precondition on update. Always populated, regardless of `view`.

content\_size\_bytes: numberÓāö

Size of `content` in bytes (the UTF-8 plaintext length). Always populated, regardless of `view`.

created\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

memory\_store\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the memory store this memory belongs to (a `memstore_...` value).

memory\_version\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the `memory_version` representing this memory's current content (a `memver_...` value). This is the authoritative head pointer; `memory_version` objects do not carry an `is_latest` flag, so compare against this field instead. Enumerate the full history via [List memory versions](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list.md).

path: stringÓāö

Hierarchical path of the memory within the store, e.g. `/projects/foo/notes.md`. Always starts with `/`. Paths are case-sensitive and unique within a store. Maximum 1,024 bytes.

type: "memory"Óāö

updated\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

content: optional stringÓāö

The memory's UTF-8 text content. Populated when `view=full`; `null` when `view=basic`. Maximum 100 kB (102,400 bytes).

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryListItem = [BetaManagedAgentsMemory](api/beta.md) { id, content\_sha256, content\_size\_bytes, 7 more }  or [BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPrefix](api/beta.md) { path, type } Óāö

One item in a [List memories](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list.md) response: either a `memory` object or, when `depth` is set, a `memory_prefix` rollup marker.

One of the following:

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPrefix object { path, type } Óāö

A rolled-up directory marker returned by [List memories](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list.md) when `depth` is set. Indicates that one or more memories exist deeper than the requested depth under this prefix. This is a list-time rollup, not a stored resource; it has no ID and no lifecycle. Each prefix counts toward the page `limit` and interleaves with `memory` items in path order.

path: stringÓāö

The rolled-up path prefix, including a trailing `/` (e.g. `/projects/foo/`). Pass this value as `path_prefix` on a subsequent list call to drill into the directory.

type: "memory\_prefix"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPathConflictError object { type, conflicting\_memory\_id, conflicting\_path, message } Óāö

type: "memory\_path\_conflict\_error"Óāö

conflicting\_memory\_id: optional stringÓāö

conflicting\_path: optional stringÓāö

message: optional stringÓāö

message: optional stringÓāö

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPrefix object { path, type } Óāö

A rolled-up directory marker returned by [List memories](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list.md) when `depth` is set. Indicates that one or more memories exist deeper than the requested depth under this prefix. This is a list-time rollup, not a stored resource; it has no ID and no lifecycle. Each prefix counts toward the page `limit` and interleaves with `memory` items in path order.

path: stringÓāö

The rolled-up path prefix, including a trailing `/` (e.g. `/projects/foo/`). Pass this value as `path_prefix` on a subsequent list call to drill into the directory.

type: "memory\_prefix"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryView = "basic" or "full"Óāö

Selects which projection of a `memory` or `memory_version` the server returns. `basic` returns the object with `content` set to `null`; `full` populates `content`. When omitted, the default is endpoint-specific: retrieve operations default to `full`; list, create, and update operations default to `basic`. Listing with `view=full` caps `limit` at 20.

One of the following:

"basic"Óāö

"full"Óāö

ÓÄ™

Optimistic-concurrency precondition: the update applies only if the memory's stored `content_sha256` equals the supplied value. On mismatch, the request returns `memory_precondition_failed_error` (HTTP 409); re-read the memory and retry against the fresh state. If the precondition fails but the stored state already exactly matches the requested `content` and `path`, the server returns 200 instead of 409.

type: "content\_sha256"Óāö

content\_sha256: optional stringÓāö

Expected `content_sha256` of the stored memory (64 lowercase hexadecimal characters). Typically the `content_sha256` returned by a prior read or list call. Because the server applies no content normalization, clients can also compute this locally as the SHA-256 of the UTF-8 content bytes.

#### BetaMemory StoresMemory Versions

##### [List memory versions](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list.md)

GET/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memory\_versions

##### [Retrieve a memory version](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/retrieve.md)

GET/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memory\_versions/{memory\_version\_id}

##### [Redact a memory version](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/redact.md)

POST/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memory\_versions/{memory\_version\_id}/redact

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsActor = [BetaManagedAgentsSessionActor](api/beta.md) { session\_id, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsAPIActor](api/beta.md) { api\_key\_id, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsUserActor](api/beta.md) { type, user\_id } Óāö

Identifies who performed a write or redact operation. Captured at write time on the `memory_version` row. The API key that created a session is not recorded on agent writes; attribution answers who made the write, not who is ultimately responsible. Look up session provenance separately via the [Sessions API](api/sessions-retrieve.md).

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSessionActor object { session\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by an agent during a session, through the mounted filesystem at `/mnt/memory/`.

session\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the session that performed the write (a `sesn_...` value). Look up the session via [Retrieve a session](api/sessions-retrieve.md) for further provenance.

type: "session\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsAPIActor object { api\_key\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made directly via the public API (outside of any session).

api\_key\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the API key that performed the write. This identifies the key, not the secret.

type: "api\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUserActor object { type, user\_id } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by a human user through the Anthropic Console.

type: "user\_actor"Óāö

user\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the user who performed the write (a `user_...` value).

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsAPIActor object { api\_key\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made directly via the public API (outside of any session).

api\_key\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the API key that performed the write. This identifies the key, not the secret.

type: "api\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryVersion object { id, created\_at, memory\_id, 10 more } Óāö

A `memory_version` object: one immutable, attributed row in a memory's append-only history. Every non-no-op mutation to a memory produces a new version. Versions belong to the store (not the individual memory) and persist after the memory is deleted. Retrieving a redacted version returns 200 with `content`, `path`, `content_size_bytes`, and `content_sha256` set to `null`; branch on `redacted_at`, not HTTP status.

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this version (a `memver_...` value).

created\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

memory\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the memory this version snapshots (a `mem_...` value). Remains valid after the memory is deleted; pass it as `memory_id` to [List memory versions](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list.md) to retrieve the full lineage including the `deleted` row.

memory\_store\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the memory store this version belongs to (a `memstore_...` value).

ÓÄ™

operation: [BetaManagedAgentsMemoryVersionOperation](api/beta.md)Óāö

The kind of mutation a `memory_version` records. Every non-no-op mutation to a memory appends exactly one version row with one of these values.

One of the following:

"created"Óāö

"modified"Óāö

"deleted"Óāö

type: "memory\_version"Óāö

content: optional stringÓāö

The memory's UTF-8 text content as of this version. `null` when `view=basic`, when `operation` is `deleted`, or when `redacted_at` is set.

content\_sha256: optional stringÓāö

Lowercase hex SHA-256 digest of `content` as of this version (64 characters). `null` when `redacted_at` is set or `operation` is `deleted`. Populated regardless of `view` otherwise.

content\_size\_bytes: optional numberÓāö

Size of `content` in bytes as of this version. `null` when `redacted_at` is set or `operation` is `deleted`. Populated regardless of `view` otherwise.

ÓÄ™

created\_by: optional [BetaManagedAgentsActor](api/beta.md)Óāö

Identifies who performed a write or redact operation. Captured at write time on the `memory_version` row. The API key that created a session is not recorded on agent writes; attribution answers who made the write, not who is ultimately responsible. Look up session provenance separately via the [Sessions API](api/sessions-retrieve.md).

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSessionActor object { session\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by an agent during a session, through the mounted filesystem at `/mnt/memory/`.

session\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the session that performed the write (a `sesn_...` value). Look up the session via [Retrieve a session](api/sessions-retrieve.md) for further provenance.

type: "session\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsAPIActor object { api\_key\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made directly via the public API (outside of any session).

api\_key\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the API key that performed the write. This identifies the key, not the secret.

type: "api\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUserActor object { type, user\_id } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by a human user through the Anthropic Console.

type: "user\_actor"Óāö

user\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the user who performed the write (a `user_...` value).

path: optional stringÓāö

The memory's path at the time of this write. `null` if and only if `redacted_at` is set.

redacted\_at: optional stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

ÓÄ™

redacted\_by: optional [BetaManagedAgentsActor](api/beta.md)Óāö

Identifies who performed a write or redact operation. Captured at write time on the `memory_version` row. The API key that created a session is not recorded on agent writes; attribution answers who made the write, not who is ultimately responsible. Look up session provenance separately via the [Sessions API](api/sessions-retrieve.md).

One of the following:

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSessionActor object { session\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by an agent during a session, through the mounted filesystem at `/mnt/memory/`.

session\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the session that performed the write (a `sesn_...` value). Look up the session via [Retrieve a session](api/sessions-retrieve.md) for further provenance.

type: "session\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsAPIActor object { api\_key\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made directly via the public API (outside of any session).

api\_key\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the API key that performed the write. This identifies the key, not the secret.

type: "api\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUserActor object { type, user\_id } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by a human user through the Anthropic Console.

type: "user\_actor"Óāö

user\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the user who performed the write (a `user_...` value).

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsMemoryVersionOperation = "created" or "modified" or "deleted"Óāö

The kind of mutation a `memory_version` records. Every non-no-op mutation to a memory appends exactly one version row with one of these values.

One of the following:

"created"Óāö

"modified"Óāö

"deleted"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsSessionActor object { session\_id, type } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by an agent during a session, through the mounted filesystem at `/mnt/memory/`.

session\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the session that performed the write (a `sesn_...` value). Look up the session via [Retrieve a session](api/sessions-retrieve.md) for further provenance.

type: "session\_actor"Óāö

ÓÄ™

BetaManagedAgentsUserActor object { type, user\_id } Óāö

Attribution for a write made by a human user through the Anthropic Console.

type: "user\_actor"Óāö

user\_id: stringÓāö

ID of the user who performed the write (a `user_...` value).

Object type.

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

ÓÄ™

BetaUserProfile object { id, created\_at, metadata, 6 more } Óāö

id: stringÓāö

Unique identifier for this user profile, prefixed `uprof_`.

created\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

metadata: map[string]Óāö

Arbitrary key-value metadata. Maximum 16 pairs, keys up to 64 chars, values up to 512 chars.

ÓÄ™

relationship: "external" or "resold" or "internal"Óāö

How the entity behind a user profile relates to the platform that owns the API key. `external`: an individual end-user of the platform. `resold`: a company the platform resells Claude access to. `internal`: the platform's own usage.

One of the following:

"external"Óāö

"resold"Óāö

"internal"Óāö

ÓÄ™

trust\_grants: map[[BetaUserProfileTrustGrant](api/beta.md) { status } ]Óāö

Trust grants for this profile, keyed by grant name. Key omitted when no grant is active or in flight.

ÓÄ™

status: "active" or "pending" or "rejected"Óāö

Status of the trust grant.

One of the following:

"active"Óāö

"pending"Óāö

"rejected"Óāö

type: "user\_profile"Óāö

Object type. Always `user_profile`.

updated\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

external\_id: optional stringÓāö

Platform's own identifier for this user. Not enforced unique.

name: optional stringÓāö

Display name of the entity this profile represents. For `resold` this is the resold-to company's name.

ÓÄ™

BetaUserProfileEnrollmentURL object { expires\_at, type, url } Óāö

expires\_at: stringÓāö

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: "enrollment\_url"Óāö

Object type. Always `enrollment_url`.

url: stringÓāö

Enrollment URL to send to the end user. Valid until `expires_at`.

ÓÄ™

BetaUserProfileTrustGrant object { status } Óāö

ÓÄ™

status: "active" or "pending" or "rejected"Óāö

Status of the trust grant.

One of the following:

"active"Óāö

"pending"Óāö

"rejected"Óāö

#### BetaWebhooks

Helpers for receiving and verifying webhook events. Use `unwrap` in your SDK to verify signatures and parse payloads; see the [webhooks guide](managed-agents/webhooks.md) for handler examples.

Possible `data.type` values:

- `session.archived`
- `session.created`
- `session.deleted`
- `session.idled`
- `session.outcome_evaluation_ended`
- `session.pending`
- `session.requires_action`
- `session.running`
- `session.status_idled`
- `session.status_rescheduled`
- `session.status_run_started`
- `session.status_terminated`
- `session.thread_created`
- `session.thread_idled`
- `session.thread_terminated`
- `vault.archived`
- `vault.created`
- `vault.deleted`
- `vault_credential.archived`
- `vault_credential.created`
- `vault_credential.deleted`
- `vault_credential.refresh_failed`

Unique event identifier for idempotency.

One of the following:

workspace\_id: stringÓāö
